headline: South Korean billionaire's risky bet pays off, as SK Hynix debuts in New York

SK ‌Group's 2012 acquisition of Hynix was viewed as problematic even within the business conglomerate: Memory chips are cyclical and capital-intensive, and the company was losing money ‌while trailing Samsung Electronics in market share and technology.  But under Chey, seeking an edge over Samsung, SK Hynix has spent more than a decade betting on high-bandwidth memory (HBM) chips, at the time a niche technology.

headline: SK Hynix Stock Spikes 13% in U.S. Trading Debut
summary: SK Hynix  shares spiked 13% to $168.49 in their Nasdaq debut as investors scrambled to get a piece of a pivotal player in the global artificial-intelligence supply chain.  The chip maker’s American depositary receipts opened at $170 on Friday and hit a session high of $177, representing a 19% jump from their initial public offering price.  SK Hynix  priced the ADRs at $149 each on Thursday.
